goal: Implement core machine learning algorithms from scratch in Python and understand how their mathematical definitions become working training and prediction code

user_context.background: Mathematically trained learner with a strong foundation in linear algebra, calculus, probability, statistics, and optimization, plus basic Python programming experience
known: Python syntax and basic data structures
unknown: Structuring a machine learning implementation in Python
Loss functions and gradients in code
Comparing a from-scratch model with scikit-learn
user_context.constraints: I prefer rigorous, implementation-focused, project-based content. Prioritize coding algorithms with NumPy and connecting each implementation step to the underlying mathematics. Build at least one complete model-training workflow using a real or realistic dataset. Avoid lengthy introductions to mathematics I already know, Python beginner courses, purely theoretical lectures, no-code demonstrations, and tutorials that only call scikit-learn models without implementing the algorithm. Scikit-learn may be used at the end to validate or compare the from-scratch implementation.
